summary_one_line: ELF header bytes that parse to a section-header table with section_count() >= 2, so the loop body is entered.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A valid ELF header at offset 0x00 (magic 7f 45 4c 46 plus class/data/version fields) whose section-table fields (e_shnum / e_shoff and related header words at offsets ~0x04-0x2f) parse such that elf.section_count() returns a value >= 2, making the loop condition i < section_count() true for i starting at 1.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: Winner-resolving seeds carry the ELF magic and structured header bytes with non-0x20 values in the class/data/version and section-count region (e.g. 02 02 03 at offsets 0x04-0x06, concentrated low integer values at 0x07-0x2f), so the parsed header yields section_count() of 286000 entry hits in W and ReadSection fires; the true side of branch 991 is taken (W resolves 10/10).
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Loser-blocking seeds fill the section-table header region with 0x20 (space) padding runs (offsets 0x0a-0x1c are dominated by 20 20 20 ... in L), so the parsed header gives section_count()==0/1; section_count() entry-line is hit only 222 times in L vs 286000 in W and the loop body (ReadSection, line 993) is never entered, so the false side is taken (L blocks 10/10).
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: cmplog's I2SRandReplace reads CMP operands from the post-execution CmpLogObserver buffer (here the integer comparison i < section_count() and the magic/header field compares inside the ELF struct reader) and substitutes the recorded comparand back into the matching input offsets, manufacturing a header whose parsed section_count exceeds 1; naive lacks I2S and must hit the multi-byte header layout by blind havoc, which it never does in 10 trials.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocked branch is the loop guard whose true side enters the per-section loop body, requiring section_count() greater than the start index 1.
cmplog resolves the branch 10/10 while naive blocks 10/10, a single I2S-delta decisive pair.
section_count() and ReadSection fire heavily in the winner and almost never in the loser, showing the loop body is entered only in W.
Winner seeds carry a structured ELF header (magic plus class/data/version 02 02 03) rather than padding.
Loser seeds fill the section-table header region with 0x20 space padding runs.
Byte-diff shows winner header bytes are low concentrated integers while loser bytes are 0x20 space runs at the section-count region offsets.
cmplog adds I2SRandReplace, which scans the input for a recorded CMP operand and substitutes the other operand at those offsets.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
`I2SRandReplace` reads the post-execution `CmpLogObserver` buffer,
picks a CMP entry, scans the input for byte sequences matching one
operand, and substitutes the other operand at those offsets.

evidence_trail.claim: naive has no I2S; it relies on havoc plus token mutation only.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: **Observed `mutation_op` in seed metadata**: any of the above. No I2S.

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: Ran `python3 tools/db_query.py lineage --branch 77 --role W --fuzzer cmplog --trial 1 --seed ac3ba2cbb00192e0`; the trailing I2S-floor signal reported 2 ancestor dash rows (mutation_op = -) at depth 16 and 17 of the 18-level chain, the cmplog/vpc-exclusive signature of an unwrapped I2SRandReplace find under the current build.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic ELF-parsing harness gating on section_count()>1 where cmplog and naive resolve the loop-body branch at statistically indistinguishable rates, showing I2S substitution gives no advantage in reaching section_count>=2.

weakest_evidence_point: The exact header field (e_shnum vs e_shoff or a derived count) that section_count() reads is inferred from source/byte-diff, not directly confirmed, so the precise input offset that must be substituted is uncertain.
